Property: Condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor for aniline; headline value reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui index (f⁺) = 2.2975867 on atom 3 (C).
Level of theory: DFT, tier standard → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI density fitting (PySCF v ? under chemkit), single-point energies on each charged state.
Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz; no further optimisation (single-point only) for neutral (charge 0, multiplicity 1), cation (charge +1, mult 2), and anion (charge –1, mult 2) states (notation: fukui // input XYZ).
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (0,1) for f⁰; doublet cation (+1,2) and doublet anion (–1,2) for finite-difference calculations.
Software/backend: PySCF (through chemkit) with Mulliken charges.
Convergence & warnings:
• SCF cycles converged for all three charge states (chemkit reports integrity.status "ok").
